blurb: Green Street Smoked Meats serves consistently excellent smoked meats in a cool, low-key industrial space where the staff makes you feel genuinely welcome.

name: Provare Chicago
insiderTip: Arrive early to grab a seat at the bar while you wait for your table—the bartenders make excellent cocktails and the bar vibe sets a great tone for the evening.
blurb: Provare is a warm, upscale Chicago favorite that delivers exceptional service, vibrant atmosphere, and thoughtfully prepared Italian and American cuisine.

name: Duck Duck Goat
insiderTip: Request a reservation well in advance and plan to share multiple dishes family-style—the kitchen times plates perfectly for a flowing, shareable experience.
blurb: Duck Duck Goat delivers inventive Chinese-American cuisine with bold, complex flavors and impeccable service in a beautifully appointed dining room.

name: Jibaritos y Más
insiderTip: Order the Jibarito de Lechon or Jibarito de Bistek—they're signature sandwiches that come served hot and quick, and pair them with the Habichuelas (beans) on the side for an unforgettable combination.
blurb: Authentic Puerto Rican comfort food with stellar jibaritos and legendary beans that keep locals coming back.

name: Connie's Pizza
insiderTip: Make a reservation to guarantee immediate seating even when packed on Saturday nights, when live entertainment adds to the festive vibe.
blurb: Connie's is a beloved neighborhood spot where deep-dish pizza and creative appetizers keep regulars coming back, all served in a warmly decorated, lively atmosphere.

name: Beatrix
insiderTip: Go for breakfast or brunch to experience their signature baked oatmeal and avocado toast in their most welcoming atmosphere — and arrive early if you want to snag their exceptional fresh-baked cookies and pastries from the bakery case.
blurb: A warm, stylish neighborhood spot where thoughtfully prepared American fare and excellent coffee create the perfect setting for breakfast, lunch, or a relaxed dinner with friends.

name: River Roast
insiderTip: Snag happy hour (4–5:45pm) at the bar for half off bites, cocktails, and wine, or request valet seating to potentially grab a table ahead of your reservation time.
blurb: A New American gem on Chicago's riverfront where crispy roast chicken and stellar service make the stunning views even sweeter.

name: Bar Siena
insiderTip: Sit on the patio on warm evenings for the full Bar Siena experience—ask your server or the owner for the gnocchi recommendation, which reviewers consistently call the best they've ever had.
blurb: Bar Siena delivers exceptional Italian cooking in a vibrant patio setting just outside downtown Chicago, where hand-made pastas and wood-fired pizzas earn loyal return visits.

name: Greek Islands
insiderTip: Ask for Yannis if you can—he gives stellar wine pairings and recommendations—and go with a group to share plates, as portions are famously large.
signatureDishes: Crispy eggplant with tomato sauce and cheese
blurb: A lively Greek taverna where generously portioned classics like flaming cheese and perfectly grilled octopus shine, backed by warm service that makes every visit feel like a celebration.

name: Chubby Cattle BBQ | Chicago
insiderTip: Arrive with a reservation to minimize wait times; order the gold or diamond tier AYCE for the best meat variety, and let your server help you cook the delicate wagyu cuts while you handle the rest on your table grill.
blurb: This Chinatown wagyu specialist delivers upscale all-you-can-eat BBQ with premium cuts that melt in your mouth and servers who guide you through the experience.

name: Girl & The Goat
insiderTip: Book reservations well in advance—walk-ins face waits—and arrive hungry to share multiple small plates family-style; the Goat Fashioned cocktail and tender pork shank are standouts worth ordering.
signatureDishes: Crispy pork shank with toasted shallots and garlic crumbs
blurb: Girl & the Goat delivers inventive, meticulously crafted dishes that balance bold flavors with refined technique in a vibrant Chicago dining destination.

name: Summer House Santa Monica
insiderTip: Sit at the bar for impeccable service and a solo-dining experience—ask for the Potato Salad Deviled Eggs and Spicy Kale Cedar Salad as a winning lunch combo.
blurb: A vibrant Santa Monica hotspot where creative American cuisine and thoughtful desserts shine in a naturally lit, Instagram-worthy setting.

name: Ricobene's
insiderTip: Order your breaded steak sandwich with extra sauce and sweet or hot peppers for the full messy, delicious experience—it's worth the effort.
blurb: A beloved Chicago institution serving iconic breaded steak sandwiches, pan pizza, and Italian classics with generous portions and unbeatable value.

name: MingHin Cuisine
insiderTip: Order dim sum via form (no push carts here) and go on Sunday morning or weekday afternoons to avoid waits—just don't expect piping hot dishes if they've been sitting, and flag down your server if something's missing.
blurb: A reliable Chinatown spot where you can skip the lines at neighboring restaurants and dig into solid dim sum and dinner dishes with generous portions.

name: The Dearborn
insiderTip: Order the truffle fries as a side—they steal the show—and don't skip the Peruvian ceviche, which arrives fresh and perfectly balanced with a nice touch of white beans.
blurb: A Chicago institution with an atmospheric dining room, exceptional service, and dependable American classics that keep guests returning.

name: Aba
insiderTip: Go for brunch to experience the chocolate babka French toast, and ask your server about ordering 5-6 plates to share—the dips (whipped feta, roasted garlic hummus, muhammara) and green harissa chicken are the standouts that justify the wait.
blurb: Aba whisks you away to a sunlit Mediterranean coast with its soaring rooftop perch, lush greenery, and consistently excellent mezze-focused cooking that keeps diners coming back.

name: Little Goat Diner
insiderTip: Make a reservation and arrive on time—the patio fills quickly, especially for brunch. Ask your server for recommendations on the rotating menu highlights.
blurb: Chef Stephanie Izard's elevated diner concept delivers nostalgic comfort with thoughtful, flavorful dishes in a vibrant pastel-lined space.

name: Au Cheval
insiderTip: Go on a Sunday afternoon (around 2 PM) to minimize wait times—or come early and aim for a seat at the bar to enjoy stellar cocktails while you wait for your table.
blurb: Au Chavel is a casual American spot that delivers on the hype with an iconic burger that's become the stuff of Chicago legend.
